mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals strengthening household demand and near-term revenue growth for index constituents, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 2.5% after recent increases signals a tightening cycle that raises discount rates and reduces equity multiple support, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.5% and is higher than 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns exceed unconditional average returns by more than 25 bps.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms broad labor-market expansion, supporting income growth and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months while UNRATE falls over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down state does not produce higher 60-day forward returns than all other labor states at p < 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid unemployment-rate increase signals weakening labor demand and rising recession risk, reducing expected earnings and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or by returns statistically indistinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Positive industrial production momentum signals improving real output and operating leverage in cyclical sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if increases in 3-month INDPRO growth fail to rank future 20-day S&P 500 returns positively, defined as Rank IC below 0.03 across two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts breakdown below 1000 thousand units signals weakening construction demand and credit-sensitive household activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below 1000 thousand units or falls more than 10% over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakdown triggers are followed by 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional median in more than 55% of walk-forward events.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices combined with a rising federal funds rate identifies an inflation-tightening mix that pressures margins and valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth accelerates while FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPI acceleration and FEDFUNDS increase state has nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 returns or does not underperform other monetary states by at least 25 bps.

mechanism_narrative: During tightening regimes, strong payroll growth when the funds rate is already rising increases expected policy restraint rather than earnings optimism, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 1 month and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with labor strength
failure_condition: Reject if this interaction produces positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 58% of triggered tightening observations or fails to underperform payroll strength outside tightening regimes.
